description: The Ñetas are a Puerto Rican prison gang founded in 1979 by Carlos Torres Irriarte in the Oso Blanco prison in Río Piedras. Originally formed as a mutual protection group for prisoners, they became the dominant gang in Puerto Rico's prison system, controlling the illegal drug trade. The gang has spread to the U.S. mainland and Spain, with an estimated 12,000 members. They are involved in drug trafficking, murder, extortion, and other crimes.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Spain, membership of the Ñetas is predominantly Ecuadorian. ... On May 11, 2014, a brawl occurred in Madrid involving Ñetas and Trinitarios which resulted in two injuries and the arrests of twenty-six gang members.
country_links.context: The Ñetas have a branch in Spain, predominantly composed of Ecuadorians, and have been involved in brawls with rival gangs.

country_links.evidence_quote: The NETA Association ... is the name of a gang that began in the Puerto Rico prison system and spread to the United States mainland.
country_links.context: The Ñetas have spread to the United States mainland, with chapters in 36 cities across nine states, and are active in drug trafficking and other crimes.
